questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: A ray of light is incident nearly grazingly on face $A B$ of a right angled prism as shown. It emerges out of face $A C$, as shown, $\mathrm{e}=$ Angle of emergence. Refractive indices of different media have been shown in the diagram. Choose the correct alternatives
options: $\mu_1^2+\sin ^2 \mathrm{e}=\mu_2^2$
$\mu_2^2+\sin ^2 \mathbf{e}=\mu_1^2$
$\mu_1^2+\cos ^2 \mathrm{e}=\mu_2^2$
If the ray just fails to emerge out of the face AC of the prism, for $\mu_2=\sqrt{3}$, angle of refraction at face AB is $\sin ^{-1} \sqrt{\frac{2}{3}}$
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \mu_1 \sin 90^{\circ}=\mu_2 \sin \theta \\ & \mu_2 \sin (90-\theta)=\sin e\end{aligned}$ Square and add we get $\mu_1^2+\sin ^2 \mathrm{e}=\mu_2^2$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A bi-convex lens is placed between a light source and a concave mirror as shown such that image of the light source coincides with itself. Then,
options: $\text { light, after being refracted, may fall normally on the mirror }$
$\text { light, after being refracted, may fall at the pole of the mirror }$
$\text { source and its image can coincide for two positions of source }$
$\text { none of the above }$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A ray of light is incident on a right angled isosceles prism parallel to its base as shown in the figure. Refractive index of the material of the prism is $\sqrt{2}$. Then. which of the following statement(s) is/are true?
options: The refiection at $P$ is total internal.
The reflection at $Q$ is total internal.
The ray emerging at $R$ is parallel to the ray incident at $S .$
Total deviation of the ray is $150^{\circ}$
questions.solution:
Critical angle $\theta_{C}=\sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}\right)=45^{\circ}$
Snell's law at points, $\left(\because n_{1} \sin \theta_{1}=n_{2} \sin \theta_{2}\right)$
$1 \sin 45^{\circ}=\sqrt{2} \sin \theta$
$\therefore \quad \theta=30^{\circ}$
At $P \because$ incidence angle at $P=75^{\circ}>\theta_{C}$
$\therefore$ TIR at point $P$
At $Q \because$ incidence angle at $Q=15^{\circ} < \theta_{C}$
$\therefore$ Partial reflection and refraction at $Q .$ As $S P \| Q R$, emergent ray at $R$ is parallel to incid,
ray at $S$.
$\therefore$ Net deviation $=180^{\circ}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: An equilateral prism PQR is placed in air with its base QR lying horizontally in yz plane as shown. A ray moving along $\sqrt{3} x+z=10$ is incident at a point $A$ on face $P Q$ of prism
options: Refractive index of prism's material for which the ray emerges grazing out of face PR is $\frac{2}{\sqrt{3}}$
Refractive index of prism's material for which the ray emerges grazing by out of face PR is $\sqrt{3}$
If refractive index of prism's material is 2, ray emerges out of the prism parallel to $x$-axis
If refractive index of prism's material is 1.6 , ray emerges out of the prism parallel to $z$-axis
questions.solution: Ray is incident normally on face. $\frac{\sin 60^{\circ}}{\sin 90^{\circ}}=\frac{1}{\mu} \Rightarrow \mu=\frac{2}{\sqrt{3}}$.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: An object $O$ is kept at $(3 \sqrt{2} L, \sqrt{2} L)$. Two plane mirrors $M_1$ and $M_2$ each of length $4 L$ are kept as shown in the figure. $n$ is the number of images of $O$, formed due to the combination of mirrors.
options: $n$ is independent of $d$
$n$ cannot be greater than 7 for any value of $d$
$n=7$ for all $d \lt 4 \sqrt{2} L$
$n=2$ for $d\gt4 \sqrt{2} L$
questions.solution:
$n_{\max }=\frac{360}{45}-1=7$

At $d=4 \sqrt{2}$ ray first falling on $M_2$ will not go $M_1$ after reflection and vice versa.
Hence $n=2 \quad d \geq 4 \sqrt{2} L$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: When object 'O' moves towards a fixed lens mirror combination, select correct choice / choices :
options: Image moves towards negative $x$-axis
Speed of image and object may be same
Image may move faster then object
Image may come closer to arrangement
questions.solution: The above lens mirror combination is an equivalent converging mirror.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A particle moves towards a concave mirror of focal length 30 cm along its axis and with a constant speed of $4 \mathrm{~cm} / \mathrm{sec}$. At the instant the particle is 90 cm from the pole
options: $\text { velocity of image is } 1 \mathrm{~cm} / \mathrm{sec}$
$\text { velocity of image w.r.t. particle is } 5 \mathrm{~cm} / \mathrm{sec}$
$\text { particle and image move towards each other }$
$\text { as the particle approaches pole, velocity of image increases }$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A luminous point object is placed at O , whose image is formed at I as shown in figure. Line AB is the optical axis. Which of the following statement is/are correct?
options: If a lens is used to obtain the image, then it must be a converging lens and its optical centre will be the intersection point of line AB and OI .
If a lens is used to obtain the image, then it must be a diverging lens and its optical centre will be the intersection point of line AB and OI .
If a mirror is used to obtain the image then the mirror must be concave and object and image subtend equal angles at the pole of the mirror.
I is a real Image.
questions.solution:
(1)
(3)
(4) Image is inverted $\Rightarrow$ It should be real
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Point $A(0,1)$ and $B(12,5)$ are object-image pair (one of the point acts as object and the other point as image) x -axis is the principal axis of the mirror. Then this object image pair is:
options: due to a convex mirror of focal length 2.5 cm
due to a concave mirror having its pole at $(2,0)$
real virtual pair
data is insufficient for $(\mathrm{A})$ and (B)
questions.solution: due to a convex mirror of focal length 2.5 cm due to a concave mirror having its pole at $(2,0)$ real virtual pair
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A biconvex lens of focal length $f$ and radii of curvature of both the surfaces $R$ is made of a material of refractive index $n_{1}$. This lens is placed in a liquid of refractive index $n_{2}$. Now this lens will behave like
options: either as a convex or as a concave lens depending solely on $\mathrm{R}$
a convex lens depending on $n_{1}$ and $n_{2}$
a concave lens depending on $n_{1}$ and $n_{2}$
a convex lens of same focal length irrespective of $R, n_{1}$ and $n_{2}$
questions.solution: The lens will behave like a convex lens or a concave lens depending upon the value of $n_{1}$ and $n_{2}$.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
